item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

flip_turn.quote: The U.S. approach of balancing individual freedom with accountability is idealistic, but in reality, systemic problems cannot be solved without addressing root causes, which often require temporary sacrifices of individual convenience.

notes: Both agents end with balance language rather than absolute support for either side. Agent A moves from an opening DISAGREE stance toward accepting temporary sacrifice of individual convenience, while still warning that unchecked societal pressure can stifle growth and innovation. Agent B remains more individual-rights anchored, allowing temporary sacrifices only within democratic process and rejecting forced compliance. Notable English-language script artifacts appear in Agent A turn 3 (印尼) and Agent B turn 6 (真正的进步).
